issues.location: bottom of screen, below the last visible button
issues.issue: The menu has 12+ buttons stacked vertically requiring significant scrolling. There is no visual indicator (scroll shadow, fade, or 'more below' cue) that additional options exist below the visible fold
issues.why: Users on mobile may not discover options like Tutorial, Faction Mastery, or Controls & Key Binds because there is no affordance indicating the page scrolls. First-time players especially need the Tutorial but may never find it.
issues.suggestion: Add a persistent gradient fade at the bottom of the button list (height: 40px, background: linear-gradient(transparent, #0a0a0a)) to indicate scrollability. Alternatively, group buttons into categories (Play, Learn, Settings) with collapsible sections to reduce initial list length.

issues.location: center of screen, two faction cards side by side
issues.issue: Both Player 1 and Player 2 faction cards are displayed side by side at approximately 170px each on a 375px viewport. The faction card text (ability descriptions, pros/cons) is rendered at roughly 10-11px and is extremely dense and difficult to read.
issues.why: Players need to read and compare faction abilities to make an informed choice. At 170px card width, multi-line text like 'Veterans: melee units gain +8% DMG per 5 seconds alive (max 4 stacks)' wraps into tiny illegible lines. This is the core decision screen of the game.
issues.suggestion: On mobile, show only ONE faction card at a time as a full-width card (350px). Add swipe gestures or prev/next arrows to switch factions. Show a split-screen 'confirmed picks' summary only after both players lock in. Minimum body text size: 13px.

issues.location: bottom of each faction card, 'No faction chosen' footer
issues.issue: The 'No faction chosen' text at the bottom of each faction card is shown even while a faction (Warriors) is clearly displayed and highlighted in the card above it, creating a contradictory UI state
issues.why: Players who have scrolled to a faction see it displayed but the footer says no faction is chosen. This creates confusion about whether scrolling to a faction means it is selected, or if an additional confirmation step is required.
issues.suggestion: Update the footer text dynamically to show the currently highlighted faction name (e.g. 'Previewing: Warriors — press Q to confirm') and change it to 'Warriors — CONFIRMED ✓' in green after the player confirms. Remove the 'No faction chosen' state once any faction is being previewed.

issues.location: center of screen, battlefield canvas area between top bar and bottom HUD
issues.issue: The battlefield canvas occupies a very tall vertical space (~450px) but during the 'Spy Unlocked' notification overlay, the actual game content is almost entirely black/empty. Even without the overlay, units appear as tiny dots near the bottom of the canvas with vast empty dark space above.
issues.why: On a 375×667 mobile screen, the HUD already takes ~280px. The remaining ~230px of canvas is insufficient to show meaningful game action when units are small. The large empty black area makes the game feel broken or unresponsive to new players.
issues.suggestion: Reduce battlefield canvas height to 200px on mobile, increase the HUD height to ~280px. Alternatively implement a zoom/pan so the camera follows active combat rather than showing the full field with tiny units. Add a subtle vignette or fog at canvas edges to indicate the viewport is zoomed.

issues.location: center of screen, full-screen overlay
issues.issue: The 'Spy Unlocked' unit notification overlay covers the entire battlefield canvas and part of the HUD with a dark overlay and large text, but provides no dismiss button or timer countdown visible in the screenshot
issues.why: If this overlay blocks gameplay input and has no obvious dismiss mechanism, players are stuck waiting without knowing when the game will resume. This is particularly frustrating on mobile where there is no keyboard shortcut to dismiss.
issues.suggestion: Replace the full-canvas overlay with a toast notification at the top of the battlefield (below the momentum bar), max height 60px, auto-dismissing after 3 seconds. Show a progress bar indicating auto-dismiss. This keeps the game visible and running.

issues.location: bottom HUD unit card grid, both player panels
issues.issue: Unit name labels under cards are truncated to ~4-5 characters (e.g. 'oldi', 'Arche', 'night', 'Grype') making them unreadable
issues.why: Players cannot identify units they are selecting or spending resources on. On mobile this is the primary interaction surface and illegible labels make it impossible to make informed decisions.
issues.suggestion: Replace truncated text labels with full names on a second line, reduce card width from ~55px to ~48px to fit 6 cards, or use a tooltip/popover on tap instead of static labels. Minimum label font should be 9px with overflow: visible or use abbreviations that are consistent (e.g. 'Sold', 'Arch', 'Kngt').

issues.location: bottom HUD, both player panels, unit card grid
issues.issue: Unit cards appear to be approximately 44-50px wide and tall — at the very edge of minimum tap target size — and are packed side by side with no gap, making individual cards extremely hard to tap accurately on mobile without mis-tapping an adjacent unit
issues.why: Apple HIG and Material Design both require 44×44pt minimum tap targets. When cards are flush against each other with no spacing, effective tap area is reduced and mis-taps cause wrong units to be deployed, which is game-breaking.
issues.suggestion: Add at minimum 4px gap between unit cards. Reduce the number of cards visible simultaneously from 6 to 4, making each card 60px wide × 60px tall. Allow horizontal scroll within the card tray to access remaining units.

issues.location: right side stat panel (BUFFS column), far right edge of screen
issues.issue: The BUFFS stat panel on the far right (showing TYPE, HP, DMG, SPD, RNG values) is clipped — the column header and values are partially cut off at the right viewport edge
issues.why: Players cannot see full stat values for selected units/buffs, meaning they cannot make informed upgrade or deployment decisions. This is a key information panel.
issues.suggestion: Constrain the entire HUD layout to max-width: 375px with box-sizing: border-box. Give the stat panel a fixed width of 80px and ensure the three-column HUD layout (player panel + tab content + stat panel) fits within 375px total, e.g. 120px + 175px + 80px.

issues.location: center panel, upgrade cards
issues.issue: Upgrade card names ('Iron Discip', 'Eagle Eye', 'Moun Comn') are truncated in the card header and the full description text below is cut off mid-sentence with no ellipsis or expand affordance
issues.why: Players cannot understand what they are purchasing. Upgrade decisions with soul costs require full information. Truncated names like 'Moun Comn' are meaningless without context.
issues.suggestion: Increase upgrade card min-height to 100px. Use font-size: 11px for card titles with white-space: nowrap and text-overflow: ellipsis, then show full name in a tap-to-expand tooltip or bottom sheet. Show at minimum the cost and first line of description (2-line clamp).

issues.location: right stat panel showing buff details (TYPE: BUFF, RNG: 45s CD)
issues.issue: The buff stat panel shows 'RNG: 45s CD' which is unconventional labeling — 'RNG' typically means Range but here it appears to show a cooldown duration. The 'DMG: 8s' label is equally confusing as DMG typically means damage, not a duration.
issues.why: Players will misread these stats and make incorrect gameplay decisions (e.g. thinking a buff has 8 damage instead of an 8-second duration). Mislabeled stats in a fast-paced RTS cause poor decision-making.
issues.suggestion: Replace stat row labels for buffs with context-appropriate labels: use 'DURATION' instead of 'DMG' for time-based values, and 'COOLDOWN' or 'CD' instead of 'RNG' for cooldown values. Consider a separate buff-specific stat template distinct from the unit stat template.

issues.location: right half of screen, entire right side
issues.issue: The game-over screen shows the winner stats panel on the left (~370px wide) but the right side of the screen appears to be empty black space, with the full stats panel for the losing player not visible at all in this viewport
issues.why: Players want to see both their own and the opponent's stats after a match. If the losing player's stats are off-screen with no scroll indicator, the post-game analysis — a key engagement and retention feature — is effectively hidden.
issues.suggestion: Stack both player stat cards vertically in a single scrollable column on mobile rather than side by side. Give each card full width (343px with 16px margins). Add a sticky header showing 'AI 1 WINS' and match duration, then scroll through both stat cards below.

issues.location: stats panel, all data rows
issues.issue: Stat rows (Overall K/D, Kills, Units Spawned, Dmg Dealt, etc.) use approximately 12px font with values right-aligned. The label and value have no visual separator (no dividing line, no alternating row background), making it hard to track which value belongs to which label across the row.
issues.why: On a mobile screen at arm's length, reading across 12+ stat rows without row guides causes eye strain and misreading. Players celebrating or analyzing a loss need to quickly parse this information.
issues.suggestion: Add alternating row backgrounds (e.g. rgba(255,255,255,0.04) on even rows). Increase row height from ~20px to 28px. Increase stat label font to 13px and value font to 13px bold. Add a subtle dotted leader between label and value.

issues.location: center — full button list (Multiplayer through Tutorial)
issues.issue: All 10 game mode buttons are presented as a flat, equal-weight list with no visual hierarchy. Primary modes (Multiplayer, Singleplayer, Campaign) are visually identical to utility modes (Tutorial, AI vs AI, Changelog). The 'Sudden Death' button uses a dark red background that appears disabled/inactive compared to the brighter buttons above.
issues.why: New users cannot quickly identify the main play modes. The flat hierarchy increases cognitive load and the dark Sudden Death button may be skipped because it looks unavailable.
issues.suggestion: Group buttons into two visual tiers: 'PLAY' section (Multiplayer, Online, Singleplayer, Campaign) with full-width prominent buttons at 56px height, and 'MODES' section (Sudden Death, Conquest, Draft, Tournament) at 48px with a slightly muted style. Add a thin section divider label. Use consistent button brightness — Sudden Death should match the others or use a lightning-bolt accent color, not a darkened background.

issues.location: center — two faction cards side-by-side (Player 1 left, Player 2 right)
issues.issue: Each faction card is approximately 175px wide on a 390px viewport. The card content (faction name, BEGINNER FRIENDLY badge, description paragraph, passive ability box, unit chips, pros/cons list) is heavily compressed. The pros/cons text wraps to 2–3 words per line and the ability description box is ~160px wide with 12px font, causing 8+ line wraps.
issues.why: Players cannot comfortably read faction abilities or pros/cons before making their selection, leading to uninformed picks — especially harmful for new players.
issues.suggestion: On mobile, show one faction card full-width at a time with a swipe/scroll gesture to browse factions. Use a split confirmation screen only after both players lock in. Minimum card width for readable content: 300px. This is a standard mobile card-selection pattern (similar to Hearthstone mobile).

issues.location: top of each faction card — unit chip row (Peasant, Soldier, Archer, Knight, Gryphon)
issues.issue: Unit chips are approximately 60×22px pill buttons with ~10px font. On a 175px wide card, the 5 chips wrap to two rows and the last chip on each row ('Gryphon', 'Knight') has its border highlighted but is visually indistinguishable from unselected chips at this size.
issues.why: Players cannot tell which unit is currently highlighted/selected for stat preview, missing a key part of the faction-comparison flow.
issues.suggestion: Increase chip height to 28px and font to 12px. Use a more distinct selected state: filled background color (e.g. gold fill vs transparent outline) with a 2px contrast border. On the full-width single-card mobile layout, chips can be 3-per-row at 90px each.

issues.location: center — battlefield canvas area between top bar and bottom HUD
issues.issue: The battlefield canvas occupies approximately 390×500px of the viewport but the actual game action (units, bases, mid-point) is confined to a narrow ~180px tall band at the very top of the canvas. Roughly 60% of the canvas height is empty black space.
issues.why: On a 390px-wide mobile screen, the battlefield is already narrow horizontally. Wasting vertical space makes units tiny and hard to follow, reducing spectator enjoyment and player awareness.
issues.suggestion: Vertically center or top-align the game world within the canvas and reduce canvas height to match actual game-world height (approximately 200–220px on this viewport). Use the reclaimed space to increase the HUD panel height, allowing larger cards and fonts.

issues.location: center — 'SPECTATING' badge overlapping the mid-capture circle and timestamp
issues.issue: The golden 'SPECTATING' pill badge is positioned directly over the mid-point capture circle and partially obscures the '1:44' game timer, stacking three UI elements in the same 60px vertical band at center.
issues.why: Players (or spectators) lose track of the capture status and game time simultaneously — two of the most tactically important pieces of information.
issues.suggestion: Move the 'SPECTATING' badge to the top bar (e.g. right of the momentum bar) or below the timer. Ensure the timer and mid-capture indicator never share the same vertical slot. Add 8px minimum clearance between stacked elements.

issues.location: bottom HUD — unit name labels beneath each card (both player panels)
issues.issue: Unit name labels (e.g. 'Peasa', 'oldie', 'Arche', 'night', 'Yohi') are truncated to 5 characters and rendered at approximately 9–10px. They are effectively unreadable at phone-viewing distance.
issues.why: Players cannot identify which unit they are about to spend resources on. This directly breaks the core gameplay loop on mobile.
issues.suggestion: Replace truncated text labels with full names on a two-line layout, or use tooltips/popover on tap. Increase minimum font size to 11px and allow card width to grow to at least 56px so 6–7 characters fit. Alternatively, remove the text label entirely and rely on a tap-to-inspect pattern that shows full name + stats in a modal.

issues.location: bottom HUD — unit card grid (both player panels, UNITS tab)
issues.issue: Unit cards are approximately 44×56px and packed side-by-side with no visible gap. On a 390px viewport split between two players, each panel is ~160px wide, forcing 5+ cards into that space. Cards are below the 44×44px minimum recommended touch target and are visually merged.
issues.why: Users will routinely mis-tap adjacent unit cards, accidentally spawning the wrong unit and wasting resources — a high-frustration, high-frequency error.
issues.suggestion: For mobile, show only one player's HUD at a time (swipe or tab to switch). This doubles available width to ~360px per panel. Set card min-width to 60px, add 4px gap, and allow horizontal scroll within the card row. This gives comfortable tap targets without reducing information.

issues.location: bottom HUD — stat panel on the far right of each player row (TYPE / HP / DMG / SPD / RNG values)
issues.issue: The unit stat sidebar (showing TYPE, HP, DMG, SPD, RNG) renders at approximately 8–9px font size in a column roughly 80px wide. Numbers like '72', '10', '58', '28' are nearly invisible.
issues.why: Players cannot read the stats of the unit they are hovering/selecting, making informed purchase decisions impossible on mobile.
issues.suggestion: Move the stat panel out of the inline HUD row. On mobile, trigger a bottom-sheet or overlay card on unit tap that shows full stats at 14px minimum font size with 1.4 line-height. Dismiss on tap-outside.

issues.location: bottom HUD — UNITS / UPGRADES / BUFFS tab strip for Player 1 (top half of HUD)
issues.issue: The tab labels 'UNITS', 'UPGRADES', 'BUFFS' are displayed at roughly 10px in tabs approximately 60px wide. On a split-panel layout each tab strip is ~160px total, giving each tab only ~53px — borderline too narrow for reliable tapping.
issues.why: Tab mis-taps will switch context unexpectedly, wasting resources or causing confusion mid-battle.
issues.suggestion: Increase tab height to 36px minimum, font to 12px bold, and ensure each tab is at least 60px wide. If space is insufficient in split view, use icon-only tabs (e.g. sword icon for Units, arrow-up for Upgrades, lightning for Buffs) with a label tooltip on hold.

issues.location: Player 1 UPGRADES tab — upgrade card labels ('Iron Discip', 'Eagle Eye', 'Mount Comm')
issues.issue: Upgrade card names are truncated mid-word ('Iron Discip' for 'Iron Discipline', 'Mount Comm' for 'Mounted Command'). Cards are approximately 80px wide with 11px font.
issues.why: Players cannot identify upgrades by name alone and have no visible cost or effect summary in the card grid, making purchasing decisions blind.
issues.suggestion: Allow upgrade card names to wrap to two lines within the card, or truncate with an ellipsis and show full name + description + cost in a tap-to-expand tooltip overlay. Increase card width to 90px minimum and set font to 11px with 1.3 line-height to fit two-line names cleanly.

issues.location: Player 1 BUFFS tab — 'Body Boon' card cost label ('💀 45') and cooldown stat ('45s CD')
issues.issue: The soul cost (💀 45) appears inside the card at approximately 10px. The cooldown value '45s CD' is shown in the stat sidebar at ~9px. Both are critical for deciding when to use a buff ability.
issues.why: If a player cannot read the cooldown or cost at a glance during fast-paced gameplay, they will either waste souls or miss optimal buff timing.
issues.suggestion: Embed cost and cooldown directly on the buff card face in two lines at 12px minimum: cost on top-left with skull icon (20×20px), cooldown on bottom-right in a distinct color (e.g. cyan for ready, gray for on cooldown). Remove reliance on the sidebar for these two values.

issues.location: lower 55% of the screen — below the two stats cards
issues.issue: After the two player stat cards end (approximately y=780px on an 844px screen), there is a large empty black region with no action buttons visible. The 'Rematch', 'Change Faction', 'Menu' buttons appear to be either off-screen or absent from this viewport.
issues.why: Players finishing a match have no visible next action. They may think the game has frozen or not know how to continue, causing drop-off at the highest re-engagement moment.
issues.suggestion: Anchor the action buttons (Rematch, Change Faction, Menu) as a sticky footer at the bottom of the viewport with min-height 56px per button and 100% width. Do not rely on scrolling to reveal primary CTAs after a match ends.

issues.location: top-left — loser quip text ('Summoners: "That was a fluke. Fight me again." (+5 bonus)')
issues.issue: The loser faction's taunting quote is rendered at approximately 11px in a muted gray color with no background, positioned at the far left edge with text running close to the screen boundary. The '(+5 bonus)' reward is inline with flavor text, making it easy to miss.
issues.why: The soul bonus for losing is a meaningful gameplay incentive. Burying it in small gray flavor text means most players will never notice it, reducing its motivational value.
issues.suggestion: Separate the reward from the flavor text. Show the quip in italic at 13px, then display the reward as a distinct badge (e.g. '+5 💀 consolation bonus') in the faction's accent color at 14px bold, on its own line with 8px top margin.

issues.location: full menu list — all game mode buttons
issues.issue: There are 10+ buttons stacked vertically but no visual grouping or hierarchy. Primary modes (Multiplayer, Singleplayer, Online) look identical to utility modes (Tutorial, Changelog, AI vs AI). The last button is partially cut off at the bottom of the viewport.
issues.why: New users are overwhelmed by equal-weight options. The most common action (Singleplayer or Multiplayer) is not immediately obvious, increasing time-to-first-game.
issues.suggestion: Group buttons into two sections: 'PLAY' (Multiplayer, Online, Singleplayer, Campaign) with 56px height buttons, and 'MODES' (Sudden Death, Conquest, Draft, Tournament, AI vs AI) with 48px height buttons, separated by a labelled divider. Move Tutorial and Changelog to a small secondary row at the bottom.

issues.location: center of screen — two faction panels side-by-side (P1 left, P2 right)
issues.issue: Both player faction cards are displayed side-by-side, each occupying ~50% of the 412px viewport (~200px each). The faction description text ('Balanced veterans. Strong melee...') renders at approximately 11-12px and the passive ability block is ~10px, making it very hard to read on a phone held at normal distance.
issues.why: Players need to compare faction abilities before committing. Unreadable text forces guesswork or requires zooming in, breaking the flow before a match.
issues.suggestion: On mobile (<600px), stack the two faction panels vertically (full-width each) with a sticky 'VS' divider between them, or implement a swipe/tab toggle between P1 and P2 views. Each panel should be 100% width with font-size minimum 13px for body text.

issues.location: bottom of both faction panels — 'No faction chosen' confirmation bar
issues.issue: The confirm bar at the bottom of each panel reads '— No faction chosen —' but is only about 30px tall with small text. It is not visually prominent enough to guide players to confirm their selection, and there is no visible call-to-action button per panel.
issues.why: New players may scroll through factions and not realise they need to press Q / Enter to confirm, stalling the game start.
issues.suggestion: Replace the text label with a full-width button ('✓ Confirm Warriors') at 44px height with 14px bold text, highlighted in gold when a faction is selected and greyed when not. On mobile this doubles as a clear touch target.

issues.location: center of the screen — battlefield canvas between the top bar and the bottom HUD
issues.issue: The battlefield canvas occupies roughly 530px of vertical height but the actual gameplay sprites and terrain are confined to the top ~180px of that area, leaving ~350px of solid black empty space.
issues.why: On mobile the game area looks broken or unloaded. Players lose context about unit positions and the match state because the meaningful visuals are pushed to a thin strip at the top.
issues.suggestion: Crop or scale the canvas viewport to fill the available space vertically, or zoom the camera in by ~2× so units and bases fill the canvas height. Ensure canvas height = window.innerHeight minus HUD height (~384px) and top bar (~50px).

issues.location: right side of screen — MUTE, FULL, MENU utility buttons
issues.issue: The three utility buttons (Mute, Fullscreen, Menu) are stacked vertically on the right edge at approximately 60×40px each. The MENU button at the bottom is partially behind the HUD area and the touch targets are narrow for the right thumb on mobile.
issues.why: Players may accidentally trigger MUTE or MENU during gameplay when trying to interact with the HUD, or may struggle to reach these buttons without obscuring the game canvas.
issues.suggestion: Move utility buttons to a collapsed hamburger icon (44×44px minimum) that expands into a modal overlay when tapped. This removes them from the gameplay viewport entirely and prevents accidental taps.

issues.location: bottom HUD — unit card labels beneath each unit icon
issues.issue: Unit name labels (e.g. 'easar', 'oldie', 'Arche', 'nigh') are truncated to 4-5 characters and are unreadable at ~9-10px rendered size. Both P1 and P2 rows suffer this.
issues.why: On a 412px-wide mobile screen, players cannot identify which unit they are purchasing, leading to misclicks and confusion during fast-paced gameplay.
issues.suggestion: Remove the text label entirely beneath the icon and instead show the full name in the stat panel on the right when the card is tapped/hovered. Alternatively, show a 2-character abbreviation at 11px bold and rely on the icon for recognition.

issues.location: bottom HUD — each unit card across both P1 and P2 rows
issues.issue: Unit cards are approximately 42×60px including the icon and label. With 6 cards per row across ~280px of available width, each card is roughly 46px wide — below the 48px minimum touch target size recommended for mobile.
issues.why: Users will frequently mis-tap adjacent unit cards when trying to deploy or inspect a specific unit, causing wrong unit spawns in a time-sensitive game.
issues.suggestion: Reduce visible cards per row to 5 maximum on mobile, making each card at least 52px wide. Add horizontal scroll on the unit row to reach the 6th card, or paginate with a left/right arrow.

issues.location: right stat panel — shown on far-right edge of both P1 and P2 rows
issues.issue: The unit stat panel (TYPE / HP / DMG / SPD / RNG values) is squeezed into roughly 90px width. Values like 'WRK' and stat numbers are legible but the panel competes for horizontal space with the 6-card unit grid, forcing both to be undersized.
issues.why: Players cannot quickly read the stats of a selected unit, reducing strategic decision-making speed on mobile.
issues.suggestion: Move the stat panel to a floating overlay or a slide-up drawer that appears when a unit card is tapped, rather than persisting inline. This reclaims ~90px for the unit card grid, allowing cards to grow to ~52px width.

issues.location: P1 and P2 HUD panels — SOULS and BODIES resource counters
issues.issue: The income rate indicator (e.g. '+2 skulls +10 skulls' seen in the top bar in sc-game) is not reflected in the HUD resource panels. Players see their current soul/body count but not their income rate, which is a critical economic metric.
issues.why: Without visible income rate in the HUD, players cannot make informed decisions about timing expensive upgrades or units relative to upcoming resource gains.
issues.suggestion: Add a small income rate label beneath or beside each resource counter in the format '+8/s' at 10px in a muted green color, e.g. `+8/s` styled with `font-size: 10px; color: #7FBA00;`.

issues.location: P1 UPGRADES tab — upgrade card titles ('Iron Discipli', 'Eagle Eye', 'Mounte Comma')
issues.issue: Upgrade card names are truncated mid-word ('Iron Discipli' instead of 'Iron Discipline', 'Mounte Comma' instead of 'Mounted Command'). Cards are ~85px wide, insufficient for the text.
issues.why: Players cannot understand what they are purchasing without memorising abbreviations, especially new players following the tutorial.
issues.suggestion: Allow card titles to wrap to 2 lines at 11px font size within an 85px card, or increase card minimum width to 100px and allow the row to scroll horizontally. Use CSS: `.upgrade-card { min-width: 100px; font-size: 11px; white-space: normal; line-height: 1.2; }`

issues.location: P1 BUFFS tab — buff cards ('War Cry', 'Iron Wall', 'Body Boon') and cost/cooldown values
issues.issue: The cost value on 'Body Boon' shows '45' and cooldown '45s CD' but the card title 'Body Boon' with the cost badge overlaps the card boundary. The '35' and '30' soul costs on War Cry and Iron Wall are readable but at roughly 10px are at the threshold for mobile legibility.
issues.why: Buff costs are critical information for mid-battle decisions. If players misread costs they may attempt to cast buffs they cannot afford, wasting time.
issues.suggestion: Increase buff card cost badge font to minimum 13px bold. Add 4px padding inside each buff card so the cost badge never overlaps the card border. Use `font-size: clamp(12px, 3vw, 14px)` for cost values.

issues.location: upper half of gameover screen — between winner banner and stats cards
issues.issue: There is approximately 120px of empty vertical space between the match duration line ('1m 44s') and the start of the AI 1 stats card, with no content in that gap.
issues.why: On a 412×915 mobile viewport this dead space pushes the stats cards down, requiring scrolling to see both player summaries simultaneously, making post-game review less efficient.
issues.suggestion: Reduce the top margin/padding in this section from its current ~120px to 24px. Move the stats cards up so both player summary headers are visible without scrolling. Use `margin-top: 24px` on the first `.go-stats-card`.

issues.location: both stats cards — all stat rows (K/D, Kills, Units Spawned, etc.)
issues.issue: Stat row labels and values render at approximately 12px in a two-column layout. With 10+ rows per card and two cards stacked, this is a dense wall of small text. Values of '0' across most fields for AI 1 make it hard to spot meaningful differences.
issues.why: Post-game stats are a key engagement hook for player retention. If players cannot quickly parse who performed better, the stats screen loses its motivational value.
issues.suggestion: Highlight non-zero or standout values (e.g. highest damage, most units spawned) with a gold accent color. Increase row font-size to 13px and row height to 28px. Collapse zero-value rows behind a 'Show all stats' toggle to reduce visual noise.

issues.location: center — full menu button list
issues.issue: The menu buttons occupy a narrow ~320px centered column in a 768px wide screen, leaving approximately 220px of dead space on each side. Each button is correctly sized vertically (~56px) but the layout feels unbalanced and wastes significant horizontal real estate.
issues.why: While not broken, the narrow column makes the screen feel like a desktop layout scaled down rather than a native mobile experience. The wasted flanks could hold helpful context (tagline, artwork, or a preview).
issues.suggestion: Expand button width to min(560px, 90vw) so it fills most of the mobile viewport width. Alternatively use a two-column grid for secondary modes (Tutorial, AI vs AI, Changelog) below primary modes to reduce scroll length. Add subtle background art panels in the side gutters on wider screens.

issues.location: bottom — MUTE and FULLSCREEN utility buttons
issues.issue: The MUTE and FULLSCREEN buttons sit at the very bottom edge of the screen in a narrow strip (~36px tall). On mobile browsers with bottom navigation bars (iOS Safari, Android Chrome) these buttons may be partially hidden behind the browser UI chrome.
issues.why: Players trying to mute or go fullscreen — especially at the start of a session — may find the buttons inaccessible without scrolling or fighting the browser's bottom bar.
issues.suggestion: Move utility buttons (MUTE, FULLSCREEN) to the top-right corner of the menu screen at 44×44px minimum tap size. Alternatively, add padding-bottom: env(safe-area-inset-bottom) + 8px to the bottom bar container.

issues.location: full screen — two faction panels side by side
issues.issue: Player 1 and Player 2 faction selection panels are shown side-by-side at roughly 370px each. Faction descriptions, pros/cons lists, and unit chip labels are all between 9–11px. The 'VS' divider and faction card content overlap visually.
issues.why: On a 768px mobile screen, 370px per panel forces all descriptive text to be tiny. Players cannot compare factions or read pros/cons without zooming, making the faction pick — a key strategic decision — frustrating.
issues.suggestion: On mobile (<900px), show only one player's faction panel full-width at a time. Add a large 'P1 Choosing' / 'P2 Choosing' header and a 'Next: P2 Pick →' button. Font size for pros/cons should be 13px minimum. Unit chips in a flex-wrap row with 14px labels.

issues.location: center battlefield canvas
issues.issue: The battlefield canvas occupies roughly 380–400px of vertical height between the top bar and the bottom HUD, but the HUD consumes ~430px (more than 40% of the 1024px viewport height). The actual game view where units fight is severely compressed.
issues.why: Players cannot see unit movements, combat, or the mid-capture zone clearly. On a 768×1024 mobile screen this makes spectating and reacting to game events nearly impossible.
issues.suggestion: Reduce HUD height to a maximum of 280px on mobile by: collapsing resource rows to a single compact bar (souls + bodies inline, ~32px tall), reducing card grid to a single scrollable row, and making the stat detail panel a slide-up drawer rather than always-visible column.

issues.location: top center — Blood Moon random event banner
issues.issue: The 'BLOOD MOON' random event notification and its sub-text ('fire half bodies!', '+1.4 skull/s') are displayed in a small centered banner with approximately 11–12px body text. The event description is critical gameplay information that affects strategy.
issues.why: Random events change game rules mid-match. If players miss or cannot read the event notification they will make uninformed decisions (e.g. not knowing bases deal half bodies changes how aggressively they should push).
issues.suggestion: Display random event notifications as a full-width banner (100vw, 48px tall) that slides down from the top for 4 seconds then collapses. Use 16px bold for event name and 13px for description. Use a high-contrast background (e.g. dark red with 90% opacity) to ensure visibility over the battlefield.

issues.location: bottom HUD — both player panels side by side
issues.issue: Two full player panels with unit cards, stat panels, tabs, and resource rows are squeezed side-by-side into 768px width. The unit name labels ('Bone Archer', 'Knight') are truncated to 4–5 characters, stat columns are illegible, and card icons are roughly 28×28px.
issues.why: On a 768px mobile viewport this two-column layout makes each panel only ~320px wide. Text is unreadable without zooming and tap targets on unit cards are far below the 44px minimum, making the core gameplay loop nearly unusable on touch devices.
issues.suggestion: Switch to a tabbed single-panel layout on viewports <900px: show only the active player's HUD full-width, with a toggle button to swap between P1 and P2. Unit cards should be minimum 48×48px with labels below at 11px minimum.

issues.location: bottom HUD — UNITS / UPGRADES / BUFFS tab buttons, both panels
issues.issue: The tab buttons ('UNITS', 'UPGRADES', 'BUFFS') appear to be approximately 50–60px wide and under 24px tall, well below the 44px minimum touch target height required for mobile.
issues.why: Users tapping these tabs on a phone will frequently miss or hit the wrong tab, disrupting real-time gameplay where fast decisions matter.
issues.suggestion: Set tab button min-height to 44px, min-width to 64px, font-size to 11px uppercase. On mobile, consider icon-only tabs (sword icon, arrow-up icon, lightning icon) with a tooltip on long-press to save horizontal space.

issues.location: far-left and far-right unit stat panels
issues.issue: The unit detail stat panels (showing TYPE, HP, DMG, SPD, RNG, and description text) are approximately 90px wide with 9–10px font size. The description text ('Heavy frontline tank. Absorbs massive damage. Iron Resolve') is completely unreadable at normal phone viewing distance.
issues.why: Players need to read unit stats to make purchase decisions. If this text is illegible, the stat panel serves no purpose and the player is forced to guess or remember stats.
issues.suggestion: Replace the always-visible side stat panel with a modal/bottom-sheet that slides up when a unit card is tapped (long-press or single tap). Inside the sheet use min font-size 14px for stats and 12px for description. Dismiss on tap outside.

issues.location: Player 2 panel — army control buttons ('→MID [NP1]', 'SURGE ×2')
issues.issue: Player 2's command buttons show keyboard shortcut labels like '[NP1]' (numpad 1). On a mobile device or when spectating AI vs AI, these keyboard labels are irrelevant and add visual noise.
issues.why: Keyboard shortcut labels clutter the already-cramped HUD on mobile where no keyboard is present. They waste space that could show more useful information like army strength or current order status.
issues.suggestion: Conditionally render keyboard shortcut labels only when a keyboard input device is detected (use a CSS media query: @media (hover: hover) and (pointer: fine)) or hide them behind a 'Show shortcuts' toggle. On touch devices replace [NP1] labels with directional arrow icons only.

issues.location: Player 1 upgrades tab — upgrade card descriptions
issues.issue: Upgrade cards ('Iron Discipline', 'Eagle Eye', 'Mount Comm...') show multi-line description text at approximately 8–9px font size, truncated mid-sentence. 'Mount Comm...' card title itself is cut off.
issues.why: Players cannot read what an upgrade does before spending resources on it. This is a core decision point in the game and illegible descriptions break informed gameplay.
issues.suggestion: Truncate card body text to 0 lines by default; show only the card title (bold, 12px) and cost. On card tap, expand an overlay or bottom-sheet with full description at 14px. Card titles must not truncate — use font-size: clamp(10px, 1.5vw, 13px) and allow 2-line wrapping.

issues.location: Player 1 buffs tab — buff card grid
issues.issue: Buff cards ('War Cry', 'Iron Wall', 'Body Boon', 'Blitz Rush', 'Soul Tide') display cooldown timers and soul costs in ~9px text. The selected 'War Cry' detail panel in the center shows a description at ~10px that wraps awkwardly across 3 lines in a ~150px wide column.
issues.why: Cooldown timers and soul costs are time-sensitive decisions. Illegible cooldown values cause players to mistime abilities or fail to use them effectively.
issues.suggestion: Increase buff card cost/cooldown badge font to 12px bold. Widen the center detail panel to 180px and set description font to 12px with line-height 1.5. Use a two-column stat layout (name left, value right) for clarity.

issues.location: center — two stats panels side by side
issues.issue: The AI 1 and AI 2 stats panels are displayed side-by-side at approximately 280px each. Stat labels like 'Units Spawned', 'Souls Earned', 'Bodies Spent' are around 11px and the numeric values 10–12px. On a 768px screen this is readable but only barely, and the unit breakdown row at the bottom is very cramped.
issues.why: Post-game stats are where players review their performance and learn. Cramped stat panels discourage engagement with the data and may cause players to skip reviewing results.
issues.suggestion: Stack the two stat panels vertically on mobile (flex-direction: column, each panel 100% width). This allows stat labels at 14px and values at 16px bold. Add a divider between the two panels. The 'UNIT BREAKDOWN' section should use a small table layout with 13px text.

issues.location: bottom — action buttons (REMATCH, CHANGE FACTION, MENU, VIEW MASTERY)
issues.issue: Four action buttons are displayed in a single horizontal row. On 768px each button is approximately 175px wide and ~52px tall — acceptable width but the font at ~12px uppercase with icon is small. More critically, four equal-weight buttons create no visual hierarchy.
issues.why: Players' most likely next actions are REMATCH or MENU, but these have identical visual weight to CHANGE FACTION and VIEW MASTERY. This slows decision-making and increases mis-taps on a touchscreen.
issues.suggestion: Make REMATCH the primary CTA: full-width, 56px tall, prominent color. Place CHANGE FACTION and MENU as secondary buttons (50% width each) on the next row. Move VIEW MASTERY to a text-link below. Ensure all buttons meet 44px min-height.

issues.location: center, full button list
issues.issue: The menu contains 12 buttons stacked vertically, each approximately 48px tall and 310px wide. On a 1024x768 tablet viewport, all buttons are visible but tappable hit areas are borderline at 48px height. The 'Sudden Death' button has a red background differentiating it, but no tooltip or description explains what any mode does, leaving new players confused.
issues.why: New tablet users cannot distinguish between modes like 'Conquest', 'Draft Mode', 'Sudden Death', and 'Singleplayer' without prior knowledge, increasing drop-off rate.
issues.suggestion: Add a 1-line subtitle below each button label (e.g., 'Sudden Death — first base breach wins') at 11px in a muted color. Increase button height to 52px to accommodate the subtitle while maintaining touch target compliance. Cap total button width at 360px centered.

issues.location: center screen, both faction selection panels side by side
issues.issue: The two player faction panels are each approximately 480px wide placed side by side with a 'VS' divider, leaving each panel only ~460px of usable space. The pros/cons lists within each card use 11px text and the faction description paragraph is truncated at the bottom of the visible card area, cutting off the second faction (Summoners) card mid-content.
issues.why: Players need to read full faction descriptions, pros, and cons to make an informed choice. Truncated content forces scrolling that may not be discoverable, especially for new players.
issues.suggestion: Reduce the 'VS' divider to a 32px vertical line with no padding. Increase each panel's inner padding from ~16px to 12px. Set faction card description font to 12px and pros/cons to 11px. Add a visible scroll indicator (gradient fade + arrow icon) at the bottom of each scrollable panel.

issues.location: top-left and top-right corners, base HP bars
issues.issue: The P1 and P2 base health bars at the top edges are approximately 120px wide and 8px tall with numeric HP values rendered at roughly 10px. During the Fog of War event overlay, these bars are nearly invisible against the dark background with no sufficient contrast border.
issues.why: Base HP is the most critical game-state indicator. If players cannot monitor it at a glance, they may lose without awareness of the threat, especially during visual events that darken the screen.
issues.suggestion: Increase base HP bar height to 14px, add a 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.4) border, and render the HP numeric value at 13px bold with a text-shadow: 0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.8). Ensure z-index keeps bars above any overlay layer.

issues.location: center battlefield canvas, vertical middle band
issues.issue: During the Fog of War random event overlay, the battlefield canvas area (approximately 1024x320px) is almost entirely empty dark space with only distant base silhouettes visible. The large centered event notification occupies roughly 200x120px, leaving ~70% of the canvas as undifferentiated dark purple gradient.
issues.why: While intentional for atmosphere, the extended empty canvas during events wastes the most prominent screen real estate and may make the game appear frozen or broken to new players.
issues.suggestion: Add a subtle animated particle effect or fog texture (CSS/canvas overlay at 20% opacity) across the battlefield during Fog of War to reinforce the event theme and confirm the game is active. Include a countdown timer on the event banner showing remaining seconds (e.g., '15s remaining').

issues.location: bottom-center HUD, unit card grid for both P1 and P2
issues.issue: Unit cards in the UNITS tab are approximately 48x80px with labels ('Peasant', 'Soldier', 'Archer', 'Knight', 'Spy') rendered at roughly 9-10px font size, making them nearly illegible at tablet viewing distance. The unit icons are also too small to distinguish at a glance.
issues.why: Tablet users cannot reliably read unit names or identify unit types quickly during active gameplay, leading to misclicks and poor decision-making under time pressure.
issues.suggestion: Increase unit card minimum width to 64px and label font to clamp(11px, 1.4vw, 13px). Reduce the number of visible cards to 4 per row and allow horizontal scroll, freeing space for larger icons and legible labels.

issues.location: bottom HUD, far-left and far-right unit stat panels
issues.issue: The selected unit detail panels on the outermost left (P1) and right (P2) edges are approximately 110px wide and display 6+ stat rows (TYPE, HP, DMG, SPD, RNG, ability text) in roughly 9px font. The ability description text is truncated mid-sentence in both panels.
issues.why: Players cannot read the full unit stats or ability descriptions, making informed purchasing decisions impossible without guessing. Critical information is hidden.
issues.suggestion: Expand stat panel width to 140px minimum. Use font-size 11px for stat labels and 10px for values. Replace full ability text with a hover/tap tooltip overlay triggered by an info icon, keeping the panel compact but accessible.

issues.location: center HUD, P1 upgrades tab card grid
issues.issue: Upgrade cards ('Iron Discipline', 'Eagle Eye', 'Mounted Command') have body description text at approximately 9px and cost badges overlapping the card bottom edge. The 'Mounted Command' card text describing Gryphon stat bonuses is partially clipped on the right side.
issues.why: Players cannot read upgrade effects before spending souls, risking wasted resources. The clipped card is particularly confusing as the cost and full effect are invisible.
issues.suggestion: Set upgrade card min-height to 110px and body text to 11px. Enforce card container overflow:hidden with a consistent 8px padding. Add a tap-to-expand detail modal for full description on tablet. Ensure the card grid uses CSS grid with auto-fit, minmax(130px, 1fr) to prevent clipping.

issues.location: center HUD, P1 buffs tab card grid
issues.issue: Buff cards ('War Cry', 'Iron Wall', 'Body Boon', 'Blitz Rush', 'Soul Tide') display cooldown timers and soul costs in ~9px text. The selected 'War Cry' detail panel in the center shows a description at ~10px that wraps awkwardly across 3 lines in a ~150px wide column.
issues.why: Buff timing and cost are time-sensitive decisions. Illegible cooldown values cause players to mistime abilities or fail to use them effectively.
issues.suggestion: Increase buff card cost/cooldown badge font to 12px bold. Widen the center detail panel to 180px and set description font to 12px with line-height 1.5. Use a two-column stat layout (name left, value right) for clarity.

issues.location: full stats panel, both player columns
issues.issue: The stats table shows 12 rows of statistics where 5 rows ('Overall K/D', 'Kills', 'Dmg Dealt', 'Dmg/Unit', 'Kill Streak', 'Mid Captures', 'Buffs Used') all display '0' for both players due to the very short 1m48s match. This creates a visually repetitive block of zeros that adds no informational value and pushes the Unit Breakdown section near the bottom edge.
issues.why: A screen full of zeros reduces perceived game quality and makes it harder to find the actually meaningful stats (Souls, Bodies, Base HP Left). Players may miss the unit breakdown entirely.
issues.suggestion: Conditionally hide stat rows where both players have value 0 with a collapsed state and a 'Show all stats' toggle link. Promote non-zero stats to the top of the table. This reduces the table from 12 rows to ~5-6 meaningful rows in short matches.

issues.location: full screen — horizontal margins left and right of button list
issues.issue: The main menu button list is constrained to approximately 400px wide centered on a 1366px canvas, leaving roughly 480px of empty black space on each side. The game title 'BEYOND RTS CONQUEST' with subtitle is also very small relative to the available space.
issues.why: At desktop resolution this feels sparse and unpolished. The empty flanking space is a missed opportunity to show faction artwork, animated battlefield preview, or recent match highlights that would draw players into clicking Play.
issues.suggestion: Keep the button column centered but add flanking panels: left side shows a rotating faction portrait with name/tagline, right side shows a mini battlefield preview or latest changelog entry. Alternatively, widen buttons to 500px and add subtle parallax background art.

issues.location: center — faction cards, pros/cons text and passive ability descriptions
issues.issue: Even at 1366px width, the faction cards are approximately 500px each with pros/cons rendered at 11-12px. The passive ability box uses 10-11px text in a constrained width, causing multi-line wraps that make abilities like 'Veterans: melee units gain +8% DMG per 5 seconds alive (max 4 stacks)' hard to scan quickly.
issues.why: At desktop resolution, text this small is unnecessary. Players comparing factions need to scan abilities rapidly, and 10-11px in a narrow box slows down reading significantly.
issues.suggestion: Increase faction card inner width to 550px each. Set pros/cons font to 13px and passive ability description to 13px with line-height 1.6. Add a subtle background tint to the passive ability box to visually distinguish it from the general description text.

issues.location: top center — base HP bars and mid-capture indicator
issues.issue: The base HP bars are approximately 140px wide and 8px tall at 1366px resolution, still quite small for the amount of screen space available. The mid-capture circle and timer text are roughly 12px. During active combat these elements blend into the battlefield and require active searching rather than passive monitoring.
issues.why: Even on desktop, the most critical game-state indicators (base HP, timer, mid ownership) should be instantly visible without searching. In a fast-paced RTS, glance-ability of status elements is crucial.
issues.suggestion: Increase base HP bar height to 16px, width to 200px, with a 2px border and drop shadow. Increase timer font to 18px bold. Add a subtle pulse animation on the mid-capture circle when it's being contested. These are pure CSS changes.

issues.location: bottom HUD — unit card labels for both players
issues.issue: Even at 1366px desktop width, unit name labels beneath cards are truncated ('Peasa', 'Soldie', 'Arche') and rendered at approximately 10px. With the full 1366px available, there is no reason for truncation.
issues.why: If unit names are truncated even on desktop, the label rendering logic may be using a fixed pixel width rather than scaling with viewport. This suggests a CSS/canvas bug rather than intentional design.
issues.suggestion: Set unit card label max-width to the card width and use text-overflow: ellipsis only as fallback. At desktop widths (>1024px), card width should be at least 72px and label font 12px, which fits full names like 'Peasant', 'Soldier', 'Archer'.

issues.location: bottom HUD — unit stat sidebar panels on far left and far right
issues.issue: The stat panels (TYPE, HP, DMG, SPD, RNG) are approximately 120px wide at 1366px viewport, still using 10px font. The ability description text is truncated. This is wasted horizontal space — the panels could be wider.
issues.why: On a 1366px screen, 120px for a stat panel is unnecessarily cramped. Full stat readability should be achievable at this resolution without tooltips or overlays.
issues.suggestion: Expand stat panel to 160px at viewport widths >1200px. Use 12px font for labels and 13px for values. Show full ability description text with word-wrap. This is a simple CSS min-width change.

issues.location: center HUD — upgrade card grid, P1 UPGRADES tab
issues.issue: Upgrade cards at 1366px still show truncated titles ('Iron Discipl', 'Mounted Comman') and 9-10px description text. Cards are approximately 110px wide, which should be sufficient for full titles but the rendering clips them.
issues.why: This appears to be a fixed-width overflow issue rather than a space constraint. At desktop resolution, all upgrade information should be fully readable.
issues.suggestion: Set upgrade card title to white-space: normal and font-size: 12px. Set card min-width to 120px. Description text at 11px with a 3-line clamp and '...read more' on hover/click.

issues.location: full screen — lateral margins and vertical spacing
issues.issue: The game-over screen constrains both player stat cards to approximately 900px total width centered in 1366px, leaving ~230px of dead space on each side. The vertical spacing between the winner announcement and the stat cards is approximately 80px.
issues.why: The post-game screen has a premium-feel opportunity — it's the moment of celebration or analysis. Dead space makes it feel incomplete.
issues.suggestion: Fill the side gutters with a subtle animated background (e.g. winning faction's theme color gradient or particle effect). Reduce top spacing to 32px. Expand stat card container to min(1100px, 90vw).

issues.location: both stat panels — all stat rows
issues.issue: Stat labels render at 12-13px which is legible but the value alignment uses right-float with no visual guide. With 12+ rows per player, scanning across rows to match label to value requires deliberate eye tracking.
issues.why: Post-game stats should be instantly scannable. Without row guides or background alternation, the data grid feels like a spreadsheet rather than a game UI.
issues.suggestion: Add alternating row backgrounds at rgba(255,255,255,0.03). Increase row height to 30px. Bold the value column at 14px. Add a thin bottom-border to each row at rgba(255,255,255,0.06).

issues.location: full screen — wide margins
issues.issue: At 1920px the menu button column is approximately 400px wide centered, leaving 760px of empty space on each side. The game logo and subtitle are proportionally tiny (~200px wide).
issues.why: At full HD resolution the menu feels empty and under-designed. This is the first screen players see and it sets quality expectations.
issues.suggestion: Expand the menu layout to a two-panel design: left panel (60%) shows a large animated faction showcase or battlefield scene, right panel (40%) contains the vertically stacked buttons at 440px width. Scale the game title to 48px font.

issues.location: full screen — faction card panels
issues.issue: Each faction panel is approximately 700px wide at 1920px viewport. While text is now readable (12-13px), the cards have substantial internal padding and the unit chip row, pros/cons, and passive description could use the space more effectively.
issues.why: At 1080p the faction screen is functional but doesn't take advantage of the large viewport. There's room for richer presentation — larger unit previews, animated ability demonstrations, or comparison highlights.
issues.suggestion: Add a unit preview pane below each faction card showing the selected unit's sprite at 2x size with stats alongside. Use the extra vertical space for a 'faction strengths radar chart' or a mini win-rate bar if data is available from previous games.

issues.location: center — battlefield canvas
issues.issue: At 1920×1080, the battlefield canvas provides ample space for game action. However, the base HP bars (approximately 160px wide, 10px tall), mid-capture timer, and soul income indicators are still using the same fixed pixel sizes as mobile, making them disproportionately small relative to the viewport.
issues.why: UI elements that don't scale with viewport size feel unpolished at high resolutions. Players on large monitors have to focus more to read small fixed-size indicators.
issues.suggestion: Scale HUD overlay elements using vw/vh units or a scaling factor. Base HP bars: clamp(140px, 12vw, 240px) wide, clamp(10px, 1vh, 16px) tall. Timer: clamp(14px, 1.5vw, 22px). This ensures proportional sizing across all viewports.

issues.location: bottom HUD — unit card labels
issues.issue: At 1920px width, unit cards are approximately 80px wide but name labels are still truncated ('Peasan', 'Soldie') at 10-11px. The cards have room for full names but the rendering logic appears to use a fixed character limit or width cap.
issues.why: At this resolution there is absolutely no reason for truncation. Full unit names ('Peasant', 'Soldier', 'Archer') should display completely. This is likely a CSS max-width or overflow hidden issue on the label element.
issues.suggestion: Remove any fixed max-width or character truncation on .unit-label. Set label to width: 100% of card, text-align: center, font-size: 11px, white-space: nowrap, overflow: visible. At card widths >70px, full names will fit.

issues.location: bottom HUD — overall layout proportions
issues.issue: Even at 1920px, the HUD uses the same absolute pixel widths as smaller screens. Each player panel is approximately 600px wide with ~360px of dead space between them in the center. The stat panels are 130px each.
issues.why: The HUD layout doesn't scale to use available desktop space, resulting in the same cramped card grids and stat panels seen on mobile, just with more dead space around them.
issues.suggestion: Add a responsive HUD layout: at viewports >1400px, expand each player panel to 45% of viewport width, grow unit cards to 90px with 14px labels, and expand stat panels to 200px with 13px font. Use CSS clamp() or media queries.

issues.location: center HUD — upgrade cards
issues.issue: At 1920px, upgrade cards are approximately 140px wide but still show description text at 10px. The cards have ample room for larger text and full descriptions without truncation.
issues.why: Small text at high resolution wastes the readability advantage of a large screen. Players should be able to read full upgrade descriptions without hovering or expanding.
issues.suggestion: At viewport widths >1400px, increase upgrade card description font to 12px with line-height 1.5. Show full description text (no clamp) up to 4 lines. Card title at 13px bold.

issues.location: bottom HUD — buff cards and stat detail
issues.issue: Buff cards at 1920px are functionally adequate but the cost/cooldown text (10-11px) and description text in the detail panel are smaller than they need to be given the available space.
issues.why: Minor issue at this resolution — buffs are usable but could be more comfortable to read during fast gameplay.
issues.suggestion: Scale buff card content text to clamp(11px, 0.7vw, 14px). This automatically adjusts from 11px on small screens to 14px at 1920px.

issues.location: full screen — lateral dead space
issues.issue: At 1920px, the game-over stat panels occupy approximately 1000px centered, leaving 460px of blank space on each side. The winner announcement text and faction quip are small relative to the viewport.
issues.why: The game-over screen at 1080p has ample room for a more celebratory, visually rich experience that rewards the winner and encourages rematch.
issues.suggestion: Add a full-width background effect using the winning faction's theme color. Expand stat container to min(1400px, 85vw). Add a large faction emblem or animated victory graphic in the left gutter. Scale winner text to clamp(24px, 3vw, 36px).

anomalies.title: Mechanic never triggered: fortune_streak_activated
anomalies.detail: "fortune_streak_activated" was used 0 times across all 276 games. This is likely a broken mechanic, not just an underpowered one.
anomalies.suggestion: Check that the code path for "fortune_streak_activated" is reachable from AI logic. AI may not have a trigger condition for it, or the mechanic may throw a silent error.
anomalies.prompt: The mechanic "fortune_streak_activated" was never used across 276 AI vs AI games. In index.html, search for the code that triggers this mechanic (likely in the AI decision function or unit spawn logic). Verify: (1) the mechanic is accessible to AI players, not just human input, (2) the condition to trigger it is reachable at the AI's difficulty level, (3) there are no silent errors or null checks blocking it.

anomalies.title: menders has severe P1/P2 split: 100% as P1 vs 63% as P2
anomalies.detail: menders wins 100% of games as P1 (13 games) but only 63% as P2 (8 games). A 38% gap strongly suggests a positional bug specific to this faction.
anomalies.suggestion: Check menders's spawn point, starting resources, or AI first-move logic for anything that differs based on player index. Also check if any faction passive interacts with map-side positioning.
anomalies.prompt: menders has a 38% P1/P2 win rate gap (100% as P1, 63% as P2 across 21 total games). This is a faction-specific positional bug. In index.html, search for "menders" and check: (1) spawn coordinates or base position that reference player index, (2) starting soul/body amounts that differ between P1/P2 for this faction, (3) any faction passive or special mechanic that checks player.id or player index, (4) AI decision logic that behaves differently based on which side the faction is on. Please implement a fix in index.html.

anomalies.title: fortune has severe P1/P2 split: 11% as P1 vs 58% as P2
anomalies.detail: fortune wins 11% of games as P1 (9 games) but only 58% as P2 (12 games). A 47% gap strongly suggests a positional bug specific to this faction.
anomalies.suggestion: Check fortune's spawn point, starting resources, or AI first-move logic for anything that differs based on player index. Also check if any faction passive interacts with map-side positioning.
anomalies.prompt: fortune has a 47% P1/P2 win rate gap (11% as P1, 58% as P2 across 21 total games). This is a faction-specific positional bug. In index.html, search for "fortune" and check: (1) spawn coordinates or base position that reference player index, (2) starting soul/body amounts that differ between P1/P2 for this faction, (3) any faction passive or special mechanic that checks player.id or player index, (4) AI decision logic that behaves differently based on which side the faction is on. Please implement a fix in index.html.

anomalies.title: plagued has severe P1/P2 split: 78% as P1 vs 31% as P2
anomalies.detail: plagued wins 78% of games as P1 (9 games) but only 31% as P2 (13 games). A 47% gap strongly suggests a positional bug specific to this faction.
anomalies.suggestion: Check plagued's spawn point, starting resources, or AI first-move logic for anything that differs based on player index. Also check if any faction passive interacts with map-side positioning.
anomalies.prompt: plagued has a 47% P1/P2 win rate gap (78% as P1, 31% as P2 across 22 total games). This is a faction-specific positional bug. In index.html, search for "plagued" and check: (1) spawn coordinates or base position that reference player index, (2) starting soul/body amounts that differ between P1/P2 for this faction, (3) any faction passive or special mechanic that checks player.id or player index, (4) AI decision logic that behaves differently based on which side the faction is on. Please implement a fix in index.html.

anomalies.title: chronomancers has severe P1/P2 split: 100% as P1 vs 53% as P2
anomalies.detail: chronomancers wins 100% of games as P1 (4 games) but only 53% as P2 (19 games). A 47% gap strongly suggests a positional bug specific to this faction.
anomalies.suggestion: Check chronomancers's spawn point, starting resources, or AI first-move logic for anything that differs based on player index. Also check if any faction passive interacts with map-side positioning.
anomalies.prompt: chronomancers has a 47% P1/P2 win rate gap (100% as P1, 53% as P2 across 23 total games). This is a faction-specific positional bug. In index.html, search for "chronomancers" and check: (1) spawn coordinates or base position that reference player index, (2) starting soul/body amounts that differ between P1/P2 for this faction, (3) any faction passive or special mechanic that checks player.id or player index, (4) AI decision logic that behaves differently based on which side the faction is on. Please implement a fix in index.html.
